tata.FY2023-24: AMBIGUOUS: FY2022-23 is consolidated while later years are standalone

sections.about: This metric measures the percentage of value chain partners, weighted by business value, assessed for environmental impacts. It is aligned to BRSR Principle 6 Leadership Indicators and also supports supply-chain risk management under Principles 4 and 5 because supplier assessment is a core stakeholder and due-diligence mechanism. A higher value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers:
Jindal Stainless moved from non-reporting in FY2023 to 16% in FY2024 and 100% in FY2025, overtaking Tata, which moved from 78% consolidated in FY2023 to 74% in FY2024 and 80% in FY2025. JSW improved materially from 0% in FY2023 and FY2024 to 35% in FY2025, while SAIL remained weak at 0% in FY2023, non-reported in FY2024 and 0% in FY2025.

Jindal’s FY2025 leadership is supported by a formal Group-level Supplier Code of Conduct and Responsible Sourcing Policy covering environmental compliance, resource efficiency, emissions control and waste management; its disclosure states 100% of value chain partners by value were assessed for environmental impacts. Tata’s 80% is underpinned by its Responsible Supply Chain Policy, supplier maturity assessments and a 4-step Sustainable Procurement Framework covering planning, vendor selection, sustainability evaluation and contract management. SAIL’s lag appears to be a disclosure and assessment-coverage issue rather than absence of environmental management: its FY2025 BRSR describes ISO 14001/IMS-certified captive mines and ESG sourcing principles, but the value-chain environmental assessment metric remains 0%.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranked 2nd at 80%, 20 percentage points behind Jindal Stainless, meaning Tata is 20% below the leader on a relative basis. Tata led in FY2023 and FY2024 but lost leadership in FY2025 because Jindal moved to full 100% coverage. Tata’s own trajectory is positive from FY2024 to FY2025, increasing from 74% to 80%, supported by supply-chain responsibility sessions and continued deployment of responsible sourcing systems. Tata also conducted value-chain awareness sessions for 325 suppliers in FY2024-25.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Jindal Stainless FY2025 reports full 100% assessment coverage; this is a major methodology/coverage step-up versus FY2024.
